items.title: community[minor]: Implement Doctran async execution
items.body:
**Description**

The DoctranTextTranslator has an async transform function that was not implemented because [the doctran library](https://github.com/psychic-api/doctran) uses a sync version of the `execute` method.

- I implemented the `DoctranTextTranslator.atransform_documents()` method using `asyncio.to_thread` to run the function in a separate thread.
- I updated the example in the Notebook with the new async version.
- The performance improvements can be appreciated when a big document is divided into multiple chunks.

Relates to:
- Issue #14645: https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/issues/14645
- Issue #14437: https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/issues/14437
- https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/pull/15264

items.body:
The changes in this PR are identical to the ones applied in the the community library  https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/pull/21561
. 

- [ ] **HuggingFaceEndpoint**: "Skip Login to HuggingFaceHub when hf token is not set"
  - Where:  langchain, parthners, llm, huggingface_endpoint
 

- [ ] **PR message**: ***Delete this entire checklist*** and replace with
    - **Description:**  Skip login to huggingface hub when when `huggingfacehub_api_token` is not set. This is needed when using custom `endpoint_url` outside of HuggingFaceHub.
    - **Issue:** it fixes https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/issues/20342 and https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/issues/19685
    - **Related PR :** https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/pull/21561
    - **Dependencies:** None


- [ ] **Add tests and docs**: 
  1. Tested with locally available TGI endpoint
  2.  Example Usage
```python
from langchain_huggingface.llms import HuggingFaceEndpoint

llm = HuggingFaceEndpoint(
    endpoint_url='http://localhost:8080',
    server_kwargs={
        "headers": {"Content-Type": "application/json"}
    }
)
resp = llm.invoke("Tell me a joke")
print(resp)
```
 Also tested against HF Endpoints
 ```python
from langchain_huggingface.llms import HuggingFaceEndpoint

huggingfacehub_api_token = "hf_xyz"
repo_id = "mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2"
llm = HuggingFaceEndpoint(
    huggingfacehub_api_token=huggingfacehub_api_token,
    repo_id=repo_id,
)
resp = llm.invoke("Tell me a joke")
print(resp)
 ```
Additional guidelines:
- Make sure optional dependencies are imported within a function.
- Please do not add dependencies to pyproject.toml files (even optional ones) unless they are required for unit tests.
- Most PRs should not touch more than one package.
- Changes should be backwards compatible.
- If you are adding something to community, do not re-import it in langchain.

If no one reviews your PR within a few days, please @-mention one of baskaryan, efriis, eyurtsev, hwchase17.

items.title: docs: script `Component lists` generation
items.body:
**Issue:** 
The current API Reference is build on the "package" perspective navigation. Each package is represented with a separate API Reference. 
The API Ref does not have the "component" perspective navigation. For example, how do you get a list of all embeddings, LLMs, tools, etc.?

With more and more components extracted in separate packages, the API Docs is also splitting into unrelated pages. As a result, it is harder and harder to overview all components from **all packages** in one go.

**Idea:** Collect classes from all existing LC and partner packages; create the lists of classes by "component" (by the component base class inheritance. Say, by the `langchain_core.embeddings.Embeddings` class for the `embedding_model` components.). Use these lists for the "component_perspective" navigation.

**PR:** created the index page generation script. It creates index pages with a table with existing component implementation classes including links to the API Reference class pages. See [result page here](https://langchain-18a4lhmdp-langchain.vercel.app/v0.2/docs/integrations/text_embedding/).
It differs from the API Reference, because it lists all component classes in one table. It does not include any helper classes/functions. But it includes classes from **all packages** in one table!

**UPD:** saved all found classes in a Yaml file. This file can be used for many helpful things. :)

langchain: support forced termination on single action agents
items.body:
### Description 
Being able to force the chain to stop is a very important feature. It helps with the flow of the chain, controls costs, and potentially controls what can and can not go into the chain for privacy. 
Right now, two solutions exist, each with its own trade-offs, as discussed on https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/discussions/22149#discussioncomment-9608308. This MR tries to give chain designers a third option: explicitly forcing a chain to return the AgentFinish action on the next observation they see. 

This is done through the following steps:

1. An agent executor (for single action agents), will have an endpoint that will keep track of run_ids that have been forced to terminate
2. A tool can potentially be given access to both the run manager and the agent executing it 
3. The tool can have explicit code deciding when to terminate and when to return a result. Said result can now be a normal result or an error string that would be used by the agent to try and fix the issue that caused the error (no trade-off with error strings and return_direct any more).

### Issues
This MR will try to address problems seen on https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/discussions/22149  and https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/issues/10714 .

### Sample code
Here is a sample dummy code for using this feature. This is a dummy example that forces the chain to stop for whatever reason the programmer sees fit (e.g., if they don't want other tools to be called based on an arbitrary flag). It can be improved if tools and agents are defined in a class (to prevent the use of things like "global").

```
from langchain.pydantic_v1 import BaseModel, Field
from langchain.tools import BaseTool



from typing import Optional, Type

from langchain.callbacks.manager import (
    AsyncCallbackManagerForToolRun,
    CallbackManagerForToolRun,
)

class PowerInput(BaseModel):
    base: float = Field(description="base")
    exponent: float = Field(description="exponent")

    

class CustomPowerTool(BaseTool):
    name = "Power"
    description = "useful for when you need to do power operations"
    args_schema: Type[BaseModel] = PowerInput

    def _run(
        self, base: float, exponent: float, run_manager: Optional[CallbackManagerForToolRun] = None
    ) -> str:
        """Use the tool."""
        return base ** exponent

    async def _arun(
        self,
        base: float, exponent: float,
        run_manager: Optional[AsyncCallbackManagerForToolRun] = None,
    ) -> str:
        """Use the tool asynchronously."""
        raise NotImplementedError("Multiplier does not support async")

class MultiplierInput(BaseModel):
    a: float = Field(description="first number")
    b: float = Field(description="second number")

    
should_stop_flag = False
class CustomMultiplierTool(BaseTool):
    name = "Multiplier"
    description = "useful for when you need to answer questions about multiplication"
    args_schema: Type[BaseModel] = MultiplierInput

    def _run(
        self, a: float, b: float, run_manager: Optional[CallbackManagerForToolRun] = None
    ) -> str:
        """Use the tool."""
        global agent_executor
        global should_stop_flag
        if should_stop_flag:
            print("Forcing termination")
            agent_executor.terminate(run_manager=run_manager)
        return a * b

    async def _arun(
        self,
        a: float,
        b: float,
        run_manager: Optional[AsyncCallbackManagerForToolRun] = None,
    ) -> str:
        """Use the tool asynchronously."""
        raise NotImplementedError("Multiplier does not support async")
    

from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI


llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o", temperature=0)

from langchain import hub

prompt = hub.pull("hwchase17/structured-chat-agent")

from langchain.agents import create_structured_chat_agent

tools = [
    CustomMultiplierTool(), 
    CustomPowerTool()
]

agent = create_structured_chat_agent(llm, tools, prompt)
from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor

agent_executor = AgentExecutor(agent=agent, tools=tools, verbose=True)

agent_executor.invoke({"input": "multiply 3 by 5 and then take the result and raise it to the 4th power."})


```

items.title: [Core] Update Tracing Interops
items.body:
LangSmith and LangChain context var handling evolved in parallel since originally we didn't expect people to want to interweave the decorator and langchain code.

Once we get a new langsmith release, this PR will let you seemlessly hand off between @traceable context and runnable config context so you can arbitrarily nest code.

It's expected that this fails right now until we get another release of the SDK

items.title: doc: fix wrong documentation on FAISS load_local function
items.body:
### Issue: #22299 

### descriptions
The documentation appears to be wrong. When the user actually sets this parameter "asynchronous" to be True, it fails because the __init__ function of FAISS class doesn't allow this parameter. In fact, most of the class/instance functions of this class have both the sync/async version, so it looks like what we need is just to remove this parameter from the doc.

community: remove redundant default arguments in HuggingFaceEmbeddings class
items.body:
- **Description:** Two members of the class HuggingFaceEmbeddings, model_kwargs and encode_kwargs, encompasses arguments of cache_folder and show_progress_bar. These arguments can be directly put in kwargs dicts and there is no need to pass them as arguments. Doing so may result in a TypeError exception if the same keyword argument is inside the dictionary. 
    The default value of cache_folder is backward compatible. To ensure backward compatibility with show_progress_bar keyword argument, the key-value pair is added to kwargs dict if it doesn't exist in the first place.
- **Issue:** 
- **Dependencies:** None
- **Twitter handle:**

items.title: Adding Managed Identity for Azure AI Search
items.body:
This PR adds managed identity functionality to Azure AI Search and the Search Retriever. Keyless authentication is part of our efforts to help make AI development more secure. More on managed identity with Search [here](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/search/search-howto-managed-identities-data-sources?tabs=portal-sys%2Cportal-user). This PR also updates the retriever notebook to show an example of using keyless authentication with the retriever. 

Twitter: @marlene_zw

Working on adding tests.

items.title: Broadcast operation failure while using simsimd beyond v3.7.7

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Related to a bug, vulnerability, unexpected error with an existing feature

items.body: - [ ] **Packages affected**: - community: fix `cosine_similarity` to support simsimd beyond 3.7.7 - partners/milvus: fix `cosine_similarity` to support simsimd beyond 3.7.7 - partners/mongodb: fix `cosine_similarity` to support simsimd beyond 3.7.7 - partners/pinecone: fix `cosine_similarity` to support simsimd beyond 3.7.7 - partners/qdrant: fix `cosine_similarity` to support simsimd beyond 3.7.7 - [ ] **Broadcast operation failure while using simsimd beyond v3.7.7**: - **Description:** I was using simsimd 4.3.1 and the unsupported operand type issue popped up. When I checked out the repo and ran the tests, they failed as well (have attached a screenshot for that). Looks like it is a variant of https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/issues/18022 . Prior to 3.7.7, simd.cdist returned an ndarray but now it returns simsimd.DistancesTensor which is ineligible for a broadcast operation with numpy. With this change, it also remove the need to explicitly cast `Z` to numpy array - **Issue:** #19905 - **Dependencies:** No - **Twitter handle:** https://x.com/GetzJoydeep - [ ] **Considerations**: 1. I started with community but since similar changes were there in Milvus, MongoDB, Pinecone, and QDrant so I modified their files as well. If touching multiple packages in one PR is not the norm, then I can remove them from this PR and raise separate ones 2. I have run and verified that the tests work. Since, only MongoDB had tests, I ran theirs and verified it works as well. Screenshots attached : I have added a test for simsimd. I feel it may not go well with the CI/CD setup as installing simsimd is not a dependency requirement. I have just imported simsimd to ensure simsimd cosine similarity is invoked. However, its not a good approach. Suggestions are welcome and I can make the required changes on the PR. Please provide guidance on the same as I am new to the community.

items.title: text-splitters: Introduce Experimental Markdown Syntax Splitter
items.body:
#### Description
This MR defines a `ExperimentalMarkdownSyntaxTextSplitter` class. The main goal is to replicate the functionality of the original `MarkdownHeaderTextSplitter` which extracts the header stack as metadata but with one critical difference: it keeps the whitespace of the original text intact.

This draft reimplements the `MarkdownHeaderTextSplitter` with a very different algorithmic approach. Instead of marking up each line of the text individually and aggregating them back together into chunks, this method builds each chunk sequentially and applies the metadata to each chunk. This makes the implementation simpler. However, since it's designed to keep white space intact its not a full drop in replacement for the original.  Since it is a radical implementation change to the original code and I would like to get feedback to see if this is a worthwhile replacement, should be it's own class, or is not a good idea at all.

Note: I implemented the `return_each_line` parameter but I don't think it's a necessary feature. I'd prefer to remove it.

This implementation also adds the following additional features:
- Splits out code blocks and includes the language in the `"Code"` metadata key
- Splits text on the horizontal rule `---` as well
- The `headers_to_split_on` parameter is now optional - with sensible defaults that can be overridden.

#### Issue
Keeping the whitespace keeps the paragraphs structure and the formatting of the code blocks intact which allows the caller much more flexibility in how they want to further split the individuals sections of the resulting documents. This addresses the issues brought up by the community in the following issues:
- https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/issues/20823
- https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/issues/19436
- https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/issues/22256

#### Dependencies
N/A

#### Twitter handle
@RyanElston

items.title: openai: don't override stream_options default
items.body:
ChatOpenAI supports a kwarg `stream_options` which can take values `{"include_usage": True}` and `{"include_usage": False}`.

Setting include_usage to True adds a message chunk to the end of the stream with usage_metadata populated. In this case the final chunk no longer includes `"finish_reason"` in the `response_metadata`. This is the current default and is not yet released. Because this could be disruptive to workflows, here we remove this default. The default will now be consistent with OpenAI's API (see parameter [here](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create#chat-create-stream_options)).

Examples:
```python
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI

llm = ChatOpenAI()

for chunk in llm.stream("hi"):
    print(chunk)
```
```
content='' id='run-8cff4721-2acd-4551-9bf7-1911dae46b92'
content='Hello' id='run-8cff4721-2acd-4551-9bf7-1911dae46b92'
content='!' id='run-8cff4721-2acd-4551-9bf7-1911dae46b92'
content='' response_metadata={'finish_reason': 'stop'} id='run-8cff4721-2acd-4551-9bf7-1911dae46b92'
```

```python
for chunk in llm.stream("hi", stream_options={"include_usage": True}):
    print(chunk)
```
```
content='' id='run-39ab349b-f954-464d-af6e-72a0927daa27'
content='Hello' id='run-39ab349b-f954-464d-af6e-72a0927daa27'
content='!' id='run-39ab349b-f954-464d-af6e-72a0927daa27'
content='' response_metadata={'finish_reason': 'stop'} id='run-39ab349b-f954-464d-af6e-72a0927daa27'
content='' id='run-39ab349b-f954-464d-af6e-72a0927daa27' usage_metadata={'input_tokens': 8, 'output_tokens': 9, 'total_tokens': 17}
```

```python
llm = ChatOpenAI().bind(stream_options={"include_usage": True})

for chunk in llm.stream("hi"):
    print(chunk)
```
```
content='' id='run-59918845-04b2-41a6-8d90-f75fb4506e0d'
content='Hello' id='run-59918845-04b2-41a6-8d90-f75fb4506e0d'
content='!' id='run-59918845-04b2-41a6-8d90-f75fb4506e0d'
content='' response_metadata={'finish_reason': 'stop'} id='run-59918845-04b2-41a6-8d90-f75fb4506e0d'
content='' id='run-59918845-04b2-41a6-8d90-f75fb4506e0d' usage_metadata={'input_tokens': 8, 'output_tokens': 9, 'total_tokens': 17}
```

items.title: community: Add IPEX-LLM BGE embedding support on both Intel CPU and GPU
items.body:
**Description:** [IPEX-LLM](https://github.com/intel-analytics/ipex-llm) is a PyTorch library for running LLM on Intel CPU and GPU (e.g., local PC with iGPU, discrete GPU such as Arc, Flex and Max) with very low latency. This PR adds ipex-llm integrations to langchain for BGE embedding support on both Intel CPU and GPU.
**Dependencies:** `ipex-llm`, `sentence-transformers`
**Contribution maintainer**: @Oscilloscope98 
**tests and docs**: 
- langchain/docs/docs/integrations/text_embedding/ipex_llm.ipynb
- langchain/docs/docs/integrations/text_embedding/ipex_llm_gpu.ipynb
- langchain/libs/community/tests/integration_tests/embeddings/test_ipex_llm.py

items.title: Upstage[patch] : Minor Fixes and Feature Improvements for Loader
items.body:
We are excited to announce the latest updates and improvements to our API. Please find the details of the changes below:

1. **Bounding Box Key and Value Update**: To fix errors occurring during Chroma embedding, we have addressed the issue where the bounding box value contained a list by converting it to a string. Additionally, we have changed the bounding box key from `bbox` to `bounding_box` for greater clarity.

2. **Metadata Simplification**: We have removed the `split` and `output` types from the metadata. This simplification aims to streamline the data and improve overall performance.

3. **Loader Example Added**: To assist our users, we have added an example of a loader in the README file. This example will help you understand how to implement and utilize loaders effectively.

These changes are part of our ongoing efforts to improve the API's functionality and user experience. We appreciate your feedback and support. For more detailed information, please refer to the updated documentation.

items.title: community[minor]: Improve InMemoryVectorStore with ability to persist to disk and filter on metadata.
items.body:
- **Description:** The InMemoryVectorStore is a nice and simple vector store implementation for quick development and debugging. The current implementation is quite limited in its functionalities. This PR extends the functionalities by adding utility function to persist the vector store to a json file and to load it from a json file. We choose the json file format because it allows inspection of the database contents in a text editor, which is great for debugging. Furthermore, it adds a `filter` keyword that can be used to filter out documents on their `page_content` or `metadata`.
- **Issue:** -
- **Dependencies:** -
- **Twitter handle:** @Vincent_Min

items.title: Fix Clova embeddings example document
items.body:
- [ ] **PR title**: "Fix list handling in Clova embeddings example documentation"
  - Description:
Fixes a bug in the Clova Embeddings example documentation where document_text was incorrectly wrapped in an additional list.
   - Rationale
The embed_documents method expects a list, but the previous example wrapped document_text in an unnecessary additional list, causing an error. The updated example correctly passes document_text directly to the method, ensuring it functions as intended.

items.title: Fix ConnectionError in HuggingFaceHubEmbeddings (Generated by Ana - AI SDE)

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Related to a bug, vulnerability, unexpected error with an existing feature

items.body:
### Fix Description
This patch improves the error handling and network stability in the `embed_documents` method of the `HuggingFaceHubEmbeddings` class by adding a timeout and enhancing error handling with try-except blocks to catch network-related errors.

### Changes Made
- Added a timeout parameter to the POST request to prevent indefinite waiting.
- Introduced try-except blocks to catch `RequestException` and handle network-related errors gracefully.
- Logged the errors with a descriptive message for better debugging.

### Issue Fixed
This patch addresses [Issue 22137](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain/issues/22137).

This patch was generated by [Ana - AI SDE](https://openana.ai/), an AI-powered software development assistant.

items.title: Add Retry Mechanism and Error Handling to Network Requests (Generated by Ana - AI SDE)
items.body:
### Description

This patch introduces enhanced error handling and retry mechanisms for the `embed_documents` method within the `HuggingFaceHubEmbeddings` class. By implementing retries with exponential backoff for `ConnectionError` and `Timeout` exceptions, this change aims to improve the robustness and reliability of the network requests made to the HuggingFace API.

### Changes Made
- Added import statements for necessary libraries.
- Created a helper function within `embed_documents` to handle retries and exponential backoff.
- Integrated error handling for `ConnectionError`, `Timeout`, and general exceptions.

### Benefits
- Improved stability and resilience of the embedding functionality.
- Enhanced error reporting for better debugging and maintenance.

This patch was generated by [Ana - AI SDE](https://openana.ai/), an AI-powered software development assistant.

items.title: community: create_sql_agent Resolve BadRequestError for API requests with pre-filled assistant

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Related to a bug, vulnerability, unexpected error with an existing feature

items.body:
**Description**: 

Fixes the issue where **API requests with a pre-filled assistant message in the final position were causing a BadRequestError with code 400**. The error message indicated that pre-filling the assistant response is not supported when using tools. The changes in this commit ensure that API requests no longer include an assistant message in the final position, allowing the API to handle the request correctly and prevent the invalid request error from occurring. With this fix, users can now make API requests smoothly without encountering the BadRequestError, improving the overall user experience and reliability of the API.

items.title: Add a conversation memory that combines a (optionally persistent) vectorstore history with a token buffer
items.body:
**langchain: ConversationVectorStoreTokenBufferMemory**

-**Description:** This PR adds ConversationVectorStoreTokenBufferMemory. It is similar in concept to ConversationSummaryBufferMemory. It maintains an in-memory buffer of messages up to a preset token limit. After the limit is hit timestamped messages are written into a vectorstore retriever rather than into a summary. The user's prompt is then used to retrieve relevant fragments of the previous conversation. By persisting the vectorstore, one can maintain memory from session to session.
-**Issue:** n/a
-**Dependencies:** none
-**Twitter handle:** Please no!!!
- [X] **Add tests and docs**:  I looked to see how the unit tests were written for the other ConversationMemory modules, but couldn't find anything other than a test for successful import. I need to know whether you are using pytest.mock or another fixture to simulate the LLM and vectorstore. In addition, I would like guidance on where to place the documentation. Should it be a notebook file in docs/docs?

- [X] **Lint and test**: I am seeing some linting errors from a couple of modules unrelated to this PR.

If no one reviews your PR within a few days, please @-mention one of baskaryan, efriis, eyurtsev, ccurme, vbarda, hwchase17.
